perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Services. VOTOCAST shall provide the Services to Licensee as specifically set forth in Exhibit A, attached hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Services. VOTOCAST shall provide the Services to Licensee as generally described in Exhibit A, attached hereto.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'specifically set forth' with 'generally described,' creating ambiguity. This implies that Exhibit A might not provide a precise definition of the services, making the obligations of VOTOCAST less clear and potentially contradicting other sections that rely on the details in Exhibit A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A SERVICES AND FEES Services Fees: 1. Implementation Fees: Implementation Fees for Branded Mobile Apps are shown in the table below: Service Fee Branded Mobile Apps $0 Admin Database $0 Website Integration (APIs) $0 Existing Mobile App Integration (APIs and/or SDKs) SO These are one-time fees, paid in advance. No work will begin until these fees have been received. 2. License and Hosting Fees License and Hosting Fees are as shown in the table below: # of Monthly Video Views Fee Up to 9,999 SO Between 10.000 — 24,999 $1.000 Between 25.000 — 49,999 $1.500 Over 50,000 $2,000 License and Hosting Fees will be billed after each month, based on the Total Number of Videos Views for that month. For tracking and validation purposes, the Total Number of Video Views will be shown within the Admin Database as a cumulative metric. 3. Scone of Services: - Branded iOS Mobile App - Branded Android Mobile App - Application APIs - Application SDKs - Admin Database Unless specifically noted othenwise, the scope of all services provided by VOTOCAST is limited to the usage and ongoing support of the Services only, and does not include analysis, operation, integration, development, additional training or modification of other custom or OEM packaged software applications, hardware or systems. Such services can be requested by Licensee and negotiated under a separate SOW. PRICES ARE STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL. NO PART OF THIS DOCUMENT, ESPECIALLY PRICING INFORMATION, MAY BE DISCLOSED IN ANY MANNER TO A THIRD PARTY WITHOUT THE PRIOR WRITTEN CONSENT OF VOTOCAST.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the services would be 'specifically set forth' in Exhibit A. The changed text modifies this to 'generally described'. This introduces ambiguity, because Exhibit A provides a very specific list of services and fees. By changing 'specifically set forth' to 'generally described,' it casts doubt on whether the specifics in Exhibit A are actually binding. Since Exhibit A lists very precise fees for specific services, making the description 'general' would contradict the explicit pricing and scope as set forth in Exhibit A.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Services Fees in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST expressly reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Services during any Renewal Term (as detined herein) but agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. If circumstances require VOTOCAST to raise its rates more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term, VOTOCAST will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase. VOTOCAST will give Licensee at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Services Fees as generally outlined in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Services during any Renewal Term (as detined herein). VOTOCAST will attempt to provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify any rate increase. VOTOCAST will aim to provide Licensee with written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: This edit weakens the obligation on VOTOCAST to adhere to rates and notification procedures. 'In accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule' is changed to 'as generally outlined' implying flexibility. Additionally, the phrase 'expressly reserves the right' is changed to just 'reserves the right'. Furthermore 'agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term' is removed, to not put a hard number into the rate increase. 'will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase' is changed to 'will attempt to provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify any rate increase' making it non-committal, and the 'at least thirty (30) days prior written notice' is diluted to 'aim to provide Licensee with written notice' this conflicts with the idea of payment schedule and makes services fees terms contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Payment. All fees are due and payable within thirty (30) days of the applicable VOTOCAST invoice. Any payments made later than the due date will accrue interest from the date due until the date paid at the rate of one percent (I%) per month on the amount due, or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less. In addition, failure of Licensee to fully pay any fees within sixty (60) days after the applicable due date, shall be deemed a material breach of this Agreement, justifying suspension of the performance of Services by VOTOCAST, and will be sufficient cause for immediate termination of this Agreement by VOTOCAST. Any such suspension does not relieve Licensee from paying fees past due plus interest and in the event of collection enforcement, Licensee shall be liable for any costs associated with such collection, including, but not limited to, legal costs, attorneys' fees, court costs and collection agency fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens VOTOCAST's obligations regarding service fees and notification procedures. Specifically, the original text states fees are 'in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A,' while the changed text says 'as generally outlined in Exhibit A hereto,' introducing flexibility where there previously was a defined schedule. Furthermore, the removal of the clause 'agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term' removes a rate increase limit. The change from 'will provide' to 'will attempt to provide' documentation for rate increases and 'at least thirty (30) days prior written notice' to 'aim to provide Licensee with written notice' directly contradicts the fixed payment terms in section 4.2 which states 'All fees are due and payable within thirty (30) days of the applicable VOTOCAST invoice.' The weakened language around rate changes and notification conflicts with the expectation of fixed payment terms in section 4.2, making the enforceability of timely payments unclear. This falls under the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall be authorized to keep all revenues derived from Licensee's exploitation of Services including. but not limited to advertising, sponsorships, subscriptions, and any other types of revenues.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee may be authorized to keep revenues derived from Licensee's exploitation of Services including. but not limited to advertising, sponsorships, subscriptions, and other revenues, subject to mutual agreement on a revenue-sharing model to be determined later.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be authorized' to 'may be authorized' and adding the clause 'subject to mutual agreement on a revenue-sharing model to be determined later,' this modification introduces significant uncertainty and potential conflict. The original text clearly states that Licensee retains all revenues, while the modified version suggests a possible future revenue-sharing arrangement, contradicting the initial statement. This could lead to disputes over revenue ownership and distribution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Licensee Revenues. Except with respect to the fees set forth in Exhibit A Licensee may be authorized to keep revenues derived from Licensee's exploitation of Services including. but not limited to advertising, sponsorships, subscriptions, and other revenues, subject to mutual agreement on a revenue-sharing model to be determined later.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'mutual agreement on a revenue-sharing model to be determined later' which contradicts the original intent of the licensee retaining all revenue. This directly conflicts with the stated terms in section 4.4, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding revenue ownership and distribution. The changed text also makes the licensee's authorization to keep revenue conditional, while the original implied it was unconditional. This inconsistency represents a strong in-text contradiction, according to the Ambiguities definition, because it introduces uncertainty where there was previously clarity.
